name: Threat Hunting Engine (KQL/Sigma/YARA)
description: Runs KQL/Sigma/YARA hunt queries against live D1 (threat_intel, scan_history, analytics_events), batch IOC enrichment, hunt-session history, and a static MITRE coverage summary.

frontend.evidence: frontend/threat-hunting.html:130,146 (Live Threat Hunt Executor). frontend/index.html:7384-7432 embeds the full section (data-auth-gate, pro-tier per line 14143) via window.thRunHunt (22228) and window.thLookupIOC (22229). frontend/threat-intelligence.html:179 and secondary widgets in frontend/cyber-defense.html:156, frontend/attack-surface-management.html:205.

navigation.evidence: Dedicated SEO page listed in frontend/sitemap.html:116; same capability also embedded on the homepage behind a pro-tier gate.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/handlers.unit.test.mjs reimplements __sig/detectIOCType as locally copy-pasted logic (comment: kept in sync with workers/src/handlers/threatHunting.js) rather than importing from workers/src/handlers/threatHunting.js — a drift-prone weak signal, not real coverage of handleRunHunt/handleHuntTemplates/handleIOCLookup/handleHuntSessions/handleMITREMatrix.

verification.evidence: First audit of this domain (previously an unpopulated [] stub). workers/src/handlers/threatHunting.js read directly (574 lines, all 5 entry points with line numbers); workers/src/index.js:7078-7119 read directly for all 6 routes; frontend pages and workers/test/handlers.unit.test.mjs confirmed by direct grep+read. handleHuntSessions requires isRealUser (line 516); all other hunt endpoints fail open to an anonymous FREE tier (rate-limited instead of gated) confirmed at workers/src/index.js:7079,7085,7091,7102,7108,7120.

notes: handleMITREMatrix's ATT&CK coverage numbers (covered_techniques: 47, coverage_pct: 25.4) are hardcoded, not computed — worth flagging if this is ever surfaced as a live metric. One of three parallel 'hunting' implementations in this domain (see CAP-TIH-002's huntingEngine.js and this capability's own runHunt) — not yet reconciled, tracked here rather than silently merged. 2026-07-11 (Tier-2 backlog item #4): fixed the frontend/cyber-defense.html:156 secondary-widget caller (not this handler — handleIOCLookup's own response shape was already correct). It read d.virustotal/d.abuseipdb directly off the top-level POST /api/hunt/ioc response, but the real per-IOC result is nested at d.results[0] (this endpoint supports batch lookups) with real field names raw_data.virustotal and abuse_score (plain number) — d.virustotal/d.abuseipdb never existed at any level, so the tile always showed 0/"checked" regardless of the real verdict. Fixed the frontend read path only; test_coverage.has_tests stays false since no import-based test of handleIOCLookup itself was added (the handler wasn't the bug).

name: Threat Intelligence Feed & Versioned API (Sentinel APEX v2.0)
description: Main paginated/tiered threat-intel feed (D1 -> KV -> seed fallback), SSE stream, manual ingest, and a v1 API surface (threat-intel, IOCs, correlations, graph, hunting) — the last three of which have no frontend caller.

frontend.evidence: frontend/intel.html:1424,1656 and frontend/intel-hub.html:470,522 call /api/threat-intel and /stats; frontend/index.html:8890,22234 pull the CVE feed; frontend/soc-dashboard.html calls /api/v1/threat-intel. No frontend caller found anywhere for handleV1Correlations, handleV1Graph, handleV1Hunting, handleManualIngest, or handleV1IOCs — backend-only, API-key/curl reachable, no dashboard UI for those 5.

navigation.evidence: frontend/intel.html and frontend/intel-hub.html linked from sitemap and homepage ('Open full Intel Hub ->', frontend/index.html:1994). The /api/v1/* endpoints are documented in frontend/api-docs.html but have no dashboard UI.

test_coverage.evidence: handleGetThreatIntel/handleThreatIntelStats <- workers/test/phase2ContractDrift.test.mjs. handleV1ThreatIntel <- workers/test/threatIntelExportEntitlement.test.mjs and workers/test/v1ThreatIntelConsistency.test.mjs. No import-confirmed test for handleGetThreatIntelEntry, handleManualIngest, handleThreatIntelStream, handleV1IOCs, handleV1Correlations, handleV1Graph, or handleV1Hunting; workers/src/services/huntingEngine.js, workers/src/services/correlationEngine.js, workers/src/services/graphEngine.js have zero import-confirmed tests.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/threatIntel.js read directly (739 lines, 10 entry points with line numbers); workers/src/index.js routes read directly (2346-3529, 3859-3981); frontend/*.html grepped for each v1 route path; workers/test/*.mjs matches confirmed by direct grep.

notes: workers/src/services/huntingEngine.js's runHunting (behind /api/v1/hunting) is a second, separate hunting implementation unrelated to CAP-TIH-001's workers/src/handlers/threatHunting.js. workers/src/services/graphEngine.js (behind /api/v1/graph) is a fourth separate graph implementation, distinct from threatGraph.js (CAP-TIH-004) and the dashboard's client-side Threat Graph and workers/src/lib/attackGraph.js (CAP-TIH-016). workers/src/services/correlationEngine.js (behind /api/v1/correlations) is a separate engine from workers/src/lib/threatCorrelation.js (CAP-TIH-017). None of these three parallel-implementation pairs are reconciled; recorded here rather than silently merged, per instruction to flag duplication rather than build over it.

name: Threat Intel Pro Workbench (MITRE ATT&CK, APT Actors, STIX/TAXII, AI Analyst)
description: The genuine classic MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise v14 catalog, APT actor profiles, composite risk/EPSS scoring, STIX 2.1/TAXII feeds, AI-generated sector/CVE briefs, and an NL threat-query AI analyst, all behind a single dispatch router.

frontend.evidence: frontend/threat-intel-workbench.html (1792 lines) calls essentially every sub-route: risk-queue:998, risk-score/cve-brief:1072-1073, actors/actor detail:1207,1266, heatmap:1329, analyst/query:1405, sector brief:1575, stix:1617,1639, tactics/techniques:1690-1691,1745. Real, complete, dedicated frontend.

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:923 — sidebar nav item '🛰️ Threat Workbench' under 'SENTINEL APEX™', linking directly to frontend/threat-intel-workbench.html. Also linked from frontend/intel-hub.html. Not listed in frontend/sitemap.html.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/threatIntelProAiRateLimit.test.mjs (12 tests, added 2026-07-11 Tier-2 backlog item #1) covers the 3 LLM-calling sub-routes only (analyst/query, cve-brief/:id, sector/:sector) — specifically their new rate-limit gating. The other ~12 sub-routes (actors, actor/:id, tactics, techniques, attack-map, heatmap, risk-score, risk-queue, stix, taxii/*, attribute/:id) and all 5 backing services (workers/src/services/mitreAttackService.js, workers/src/services/aptActorProfiles.js, workers/src/services/compositeRiskScoring.js, workers/src/services/stix21Engine.js, workers/src/services/aiThreatAnalyst.js's internals beyond the mocked call boundary) remain untested — still the single highest-value test-writing target in this domain by surface area.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/threatIntelPro.js read directly (now 581 lines after the 2026-07-11 rate-limit fix, single router handleThreatIntelPro:72 dispatching ~15 internal sub-paths by path match); workers/src/index.js:5251-5273 read directly (single prefix dispatch block, routes_fully_enumerated correctly set false per docs/capability-registry/SCHEMA.md's own convention for this pattern); frontend/threat-intel-workbench.html read directly for all call sites; workers/test/threatIntelProAiRateLimit.test.mjs confirmed via direct run (12/12 passing).

notes: Real, complete, prominently-discoverable feature. Sub-routes are individually tier-gated inside their handlers (e.g. STIX actors at ENTERPRISE, IOCs at PRO+) rather than at the router level, so rbac.enforced is marked "unknown" pending a full per-sub-route trace. 2026-07-11 (Tier-2 backlog item #1): the 3 sub-routes that make a real LLM call (analyst/query chat, AI-generated cve-brief, AI-generated sector brief) had zero authentication requirement AND zero rate limiting — any anonymous visitor could script unlimited calls, each one billed as real third-party LLM API usage; an open cost-abuse vector. Fixed by wiring the existing shared checkRateLimitCost() (workers/src/middleware/rateLimit.js — already used by threatHunting.js/vulnManagement.js/auditLog.js for their own costly operations) into all 3 routes, with 3 new ENDPOINT_COST entries (intel/analyst, intel/cve-brief, intel/sector, each cost 2). auth_enforced intentionally still false — this closes the cost-abuse gap via per-IP/per-tier quota, not a login requirement change; FREE-tier/anonymous callers get a capped daily budget (~5 calls/day at cost 2 each under TIERS.FREE's 10-unit budget) instead of unlimited access. The other ~12 sub-routes were not in scope for this fix (they don't call an LLM and were not part of the audit finding) and remain exactly as previously documented. FIXED 2026-07-12 (Tier-3 backlog item #6, last of 6, full-frontend-audit follow-up — this closes the entire 24-item backlog): the APT Actor Profiles panel's actor cards (grid + detail modal) rendered a raw keyword string ('bear', 'dragon', 'typhoon', …) crammed into a 36px icon box instead of an emoji. workers/src/services/aptActorProfiles.js's `icon` field is a semantic keyword, not a display glyph, and every one of the 15 actors in APT_ACTORS has it set — so frontend/threat-intel-workbench.html's `${a.icon || '🎭'}` fallback never actually triggered for any real actor; it always printed the literal keyword text. Frontend-only fix: added an `ACTOR_ICON_EMOJI` keyword→glyph map plus an `actorIconEmoji(a)` helper (falling back to 🎭 for any unmapped keyword, preserving the original degrade-gracefully intent), used by both the grid card and the detail modal. No backend change needed — the semantic-keyword data design is reasonable; the gap was purely the missing frontend translation step. New workers/test/threatIntelWorkbenchActorIconFix.test.mjs (5 tests): a real-data contract test importing APT_ACTORS directly and asserting every actor's real icon keyword is covered by the frontend's map (the general form of this bug — an unmapped keyword — not just the one instance), plus static-parse checks on both render sites. test_coverage.has_tests above unchanged (frontend-only static parse plus a data-shape assertion, not new coverage of handleThreatIntelPro itself). Full suite: 244 files / 2489 tests passing.

name: Threat Intelligence Graph (backend) — distinct from the dashboard's client-side Threat Graph
description: A curated static knowledge base (APT groups, malware, CVEs, ATT&CK techniques, sectors) with live D1 augmentation and CISA-KEV enrichment, plus node/path/query/summary sub-routes (BFS shortest attack path). Has zero frontend callers.

frontend.evidence: Exhaustive grep of every frontend/*.html file for the literal string /api/threat-graph returns zero matches. This backend capability has no dedicated page and is not called from any dashboard.

navigation.evidence: No page reaches this backend at all. Note: frontend/user-dashboard.html DOES have a nav item and page called 'Threat Graph' (line 899-901, #page-threatgraph), but its initThreatGraph() (line 3802) builds nodes/edges entirely client-side from the logged-in user's own _allScans history with zero calls to any /api/threat-graph* endpoint — a completely separate feature sharing only a name (see notes).

test_coverage.evidence: handleGetThreatGraph only <- workers/test/threatIntelCanonicalColumns.test.mjs (imports just this one function). handleGetGraphNodes/handleGetGraphPaths/handleGraphQuery/handleGraphSummary have no import-confirmed tests.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/threatGraph.js read directly (450 lines, all 5 entry points); workers/src/index.js:4352-4370 read directly — only the root route resolves authCtx at all (fails open to {}), nodes/paths/query/summary take no auth parameter whatsoever; frontend/*.html grepped exhaustively for '/api/threat-graph' (0 hits).

notes: Important disambiguation for anyone working this domain: this backend capability and user-dashboard.html's client-side 'Threat Graph' feature are two entirely independent systems sharing only a name. The dashboard's client-side graph is already tracked and partially fixed this session (docs/capability-registry/domains/organizations.json-adjacent Threat Graph findings-persistence work, PR #159/160): scan_history never persisted a findings column, so every real scan produced exactly one isolated node with no relationship edges. workers/schema_migration_scan_history_findings_2026_07.sql and workers/src/lib/findingsSummary.js shipped as a safe no-op fix for that gap but the migration has not yet been applied to production (owner must run .github/workflows/db-migrate.yml with APPLY). That fix is unrelated to and does not touch this backend capability (threatGraph.js), which remains entirely unused by any frontend.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/attackLibrary.js read directly (195 lines); schema_v44_attack_library.sql and schema_v44b_attack_library_categories.sql read directly, confirming this catalogs MITRE ATLAS (e.g. AML.T0054) + OWASP LLM Top-10, not classic ATT&CK; workers/src/index.js:7927-7936 read directly; frontend/attack-library.html read directly; workers/test/attackLibrary.test.mjs confirmed by direct grep.

Do not confuse with classic MITRE ATT&CK (CAP-TIH-003) despite the schema-version-number adjacency to unrelated v44 migrations.

navigation.evidence: Linked from BOTH desktop and mobile primary nav on frontend/index.html, labeled '🤖 AI Threat Intel' (lines 1180, 1223), plus in-page CTAs (2022, 5595-5596, 5617, 12300) and frontend/sitemap.html:142 — the most prominently-linked page in this domain.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/agentThreatAdvisories.js read directly (223 lines); workers/src/index.js:7909-7919 read directly, admin POSTs gated by isAdminAuthorized() inline check (7919); frontend/agent-threats.html and frontend/index.html nav entries read directly; workers/test/agentThreatAdvisories.test.mjs confirmed by direct grep.

Sole gap is agentThreatIngestion.js itself lacking direct test coverage. Not marked GA APPROVED outright because that operational_status value mechanically requires customer_journey_complete:true (docs/capability-registry/SCHEMA.md §5.5), which requires a dynamic_browser verification pass not yet performed for this domain.

name: AI Threat Intelligence Feed & Agent Security (Pillar 4/5)
description: Curated + live-ingested (NVD/CISA-KEV/OSV.dev/GitHub Advisories) AI-CVE and prompt-attack feed, a premium 12-section markdown intelligence report, threat radar status/scan-now, and an AI-agent inventory scanner.

frontend.evidence: frontend/index.html embeds an 'AI Threat Intel' panel (renderAIThreatIntelFlagship, auto-refresh every 90s matching the handler's own autoRefreshSec:90 contract). frontend/agent-threats.html has a dedicated 'Scan My Agent' modal (runAgentScan(), 2026-07-11) that POSTs to /api/ai-security/agents/scan — the handleScanAgent entry point specifically.

navigation.evidence: Embedded homepage widget only; not independently confirmed to have its own nav entry distinct from the homepage itself.

test_coverage.evidence: handleAIThreatFeed/handleAIThreatRadarScanNow <- workers/test/aiThreatIntel.test.mjs. workers/src/services/aiThreatIngestion.js <- workers/test/aiThreatIngestion.test.mjs. workers/src/services/aiThreatRadar.js <- workers/test/aiThreatRadar.test.mjs and workers/test/aiThreatIntel.test.mjs. Good coverage relative to the rest of this domain.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/aiThreatIntel.js read (1011 lines, read to line 825; core exports confirmed via import statements). workers/src/index.js routes 7700-7725 read directly. Freemium gating confirmed: FREE_LIVE_PREVIEW=3 live entries plus full curated library for anonymous callers, no isRealUser hard gate. workers/test/*.mjs matches confirmed by direct grep.

notes: Priority P3 (navigation partly unconfirmed) rather than lower, since this is otherwise one of the better-built and better-tested capabilities in the domain — the gap is specifically not having verified where/how a customer discovers it beyond the homepage embed. auth_enforced:false describes the feed/report/radar entry points only (confirmed genuinely public, no isRealUser gate) — it does NOT apply to handleScanAgent/handleRegisterAgent/handleListAgents, all 3 of which DO hard-require a real authenticated user (`if (!authCtx?.userId) return err('Auth required', 401)`), a nuance this single top-level field can't capture for a capability bundling entry points with different auth requirements. 2026-07-11 (Tier-2 backlog item #7): found and fixed the real consequence of that nuance — frontend/agent-threats.html's runAgentScan() sent zero auth header of any kind, so handleScanAgent always 401'd for every visitor including logged-in customers. The bug was invisible in the UI because the page has a fully-functional client-side fallback (an identical OWASP LLM Top 10 rule engine) that always produced a complete-looking report — but the real, D1-persisted assessment (real agent_id, actually retrievable via handleListAgents, actually eligible for 'Register & Save Report' / advisory alerts / rescans) never fired for anyone. Fixed by adding a getAuthToken() helper (sessionStorage['cdb_access'] first, matching the convention already established elsewhere this session) and attaching it as a Bearer Authorization header.

description: Aggregates ~30 global OSINT sources into a tiered feed, briefing, and source list; a manual refresh trigger is owner/admin-gated to prevent anonymous triggering of the expensive pipeline.

navigation.evidence: Not in top-level nav or sitemap; reachable only via frontend/intel-hub.html.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/globalIntel.js read directly (279 lines); workers/src/index.js:7945-7955 read directly; handleGlobalIntelRefresh's isOwner(authCtx) OR ADMIN_TOKEN gate confirmed at workers/src/handlers/globalIntel.js:259-274; GI_PLANS per-tier item caps (FREE 6/no IOCs up to MSSP 200) confirmed; workers/test/*.mjs matches confirmed by direct grep.

name: Threat Intelligence API Economy (monetized IOC/CVE/Actor/TTP/Risk)
description: A pure API-economy product for external integrators — IOC, CVE, actor, TTP, and risk lookups with per-endpoint tier gating, D1 entitlement checks, and dual daily+per-minute rate limiting. Documented in API docs only; no dashboard UI.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-12: frontend/user-dashboard.html gained a real "Threat Intel API" tab (id="page-intel-api") with 5 live lookup tools, one per real endpoint (IOC check, CVE lookup, threat actor search, MITRE ATT&CK TTP lookup, composite risk score) — each calls the real GET /api/intel/* route and renders the real response (verdict/risk_score for IOC, CVSS/KEV/severity for CVE, actor profile or list, technique+mitigations for TTP, composite score+level+narrative for risk). A tier/quota status line at the top shows the customer's plan and today's usage. Because access is tiered per-endpoint (FREE/STARTER: ioc+cve only; PRO/ENTERPRISE: all 5) rather than an all-or-nothing gate, a 429 from any individual tool renders an inline upgrade notice for that tool specifically rather than blocking the whole page. Before this fix: no frontend/*.html page called any /api/intel/{ioc,cve,actor,ttp,risk} route — documented in frontend/api-docs.html text only, no interactive UI.

navigation.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-12: frontend/user-dashboard.html's sidebar (same section as DPDP/Compliance/CISO Metrics) now has a real nav-item (data-page="intel-api") linking to the new tab.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/intelApiDashboard.test.mjs (2026-07-12, 11 tests) — closes the test-coverage gap for all 4 previously-unconfirmed handlers: handleIntelCVE (honest found_in_db:false for an unknown CVE, no hallucination), handleIntelActor (honest empty result set, not fabricated), handleIntelTTP (real embedded MITRE catalog lookup), handleIntelRisk (real composite score from real component weights), plus handleIntelIOC and a 429/upgrade-url check for a FREE-tier caller. Frontend tests confirm the new tab's nav-item, 5 tool inputs, real endpoint calls per tool, and inline (not page-blocking) 429 handling.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/intelAPIHandlers.js read directly (661 lines, 5 entry points with line numbers, handleIntelTTP's own embedded 30-technique MITRE catalog noted as a third parallel ATT&CK representation); workers/src/index.js:5229-5245 read directly; tierLimits()/checkEntitlement()/dual rate-limiting confirmed. FIXED 2026-07-12 (22-paid-feature program, item 8 of 22, owner-directed build): frontend/user-dashboard.html read and edited directly to add the real Threat Intel API tab described in frontend.evidence above. All 3 inline <script> blocks re-parsed with node --check (syntax valid). No backend change. Full suite green: 250 files / 2549 tests. Registry validator: 0 failures, 0 warnings.

notes: The prior wave's open question — 'worth a decision on whether [no dashboard] is acceptable for an API-only SKU or a real gap' — is now resolved by explicit owner direction: build a real customer-facing dashboard. Done; see frontend.evidence. handleIntelTTP's embedded 30-technique MITRE catalog remains a noted third parallel ATT&CK representation on this platform (alongside at least two others catalogued elsewhere in this domain) — not reconciled by this fix, which only added a UI consumer for the existing handler, per this wave's standing discipline to flag duplication rather than silently merge engines outside the scope of the specific fix at hand.

name: Intelligence Preview (freemium teaser cards)
description: A fully-built, tier-gated conversion-funnel feature (CVE/threat-actor/malware-family preview cards, IOC sample, report sample, catalog, featured, unlock) referencing an EXTERNAL storefront's pricing/upgrade URLs rather than this platform's own /pricing — and has zero frontend callers in this codebase.

frontend.evidence: Exhaustive grep of frontend/*.html for '/api/preview/' returns zero matches. A fully-built, tier-gated feature with no wiring into this codebase's own frontend at all — its upgrade URLs point to an external storefront (intel.cyberdudebivash.com), not this platform's own /pricing, suggesting it may have been built for a different property or channel.

navigation.evidence: No page, no nav entry — entirely unreachable from this codebase's frontend.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/intelligencePreview.js read directly (722 lines, single router dispatching 8 sub-actions); isPremium() JWT/D1-entitlement check and 10/min KV rate limit for FREE authenticated users confirmed; workers/src/index.js:7557-7558 read directly (single lazy-import prefix dispatch); frontend/*.html grepped exhaustively for '/api/preview/' (0 hits).

notes: Before building any frontend for this, confirm with the business owner whether it targets this platform's own conversion funnel or was built for/copied from a different storefront (intel.cyberdudebivash.com) — the external upgrade URLs are a real, concrete signal this may be intentionally out of scope for this codebase's frontend, not simply an unwired backend. Do not build a UI here without that confirmation, per this wave's duplicate/orphan reconciliation-before-building discipline.

name: Public Threat-Intel Feeds & Monetization Tier Matrix
description: A family of public, versioned JSON feeds (feed.json, v1/intel/latest, apex, ai_summary, kev, stix, pricing, reports/latest) driven by a shared tier/pricing/gating engine. The best-tested capability in this domain.

frontend.evidence: frontend/threat-intelligence.html:323 documents the KEV feed route in prose as an example, not as a live fetch() call.

navigation.evidence: Documented in prose only, not a live in-app UI flow.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/publicFeeds.js and workers/src/handlers/intelMonetization.js read directly; workers/src/index.js:1472-1484 read directly (single routing block for all 8 feed paths); frontend/threat-intelligence.html:323 read directly (prose reference, not a live call); all 5 workers/test/*.mjs matches confirmed by direct grep+read.

notes: This is an API-economy product by design (external integrators pull these JSON feeds directly), so 'no live in-app UI call' is expected rather than a defect — set to P3 (navigation/discoverability of the product itself, e.g. whether customers can find and use their API key against these feeds from within the dashboard) rather than P2, since the backend+test story here is the strongest in the whole domain.

name: Attack Graph from Scan Results
description: Builds a D3-ready force-directed graph from a single completed scan result (not from the threat-intel corpus) — distinct from CAP-TIH-004's threat-graph backend and from workers/src/services/graphEngine.js (CAP-TIH-002).

frontend.evidence: Referenced in frontend/index.html's PROD_FEATURES doc array and frontend/enterprise-portal.html:249 lists it as a feature/endpoint, but no live fetch('/api/attack-graph'...) call was found in any frontend file in this pass — likely called from a post-scan results view, not independently confirmed as live-wired.

navigation.evidence: Not independently traced to a specific nav path in this pass.

test_coverage.evidence: No import-confirmed test found for workers/src/lib/attackGraph.js.

verification.evidence: workers/src/lib/attackGraph.js read directly (396 lines); workers/src/index.js:2323-2338 read directly, inline comment confirms 'public by design, rate-limited instead of tier-gated so the free scan-results UI keeps working' (lines 2320-2322); also consumed by workers/src/handlers/monitoring.js; frontend/index.html and frontend/enterprise-portal.html:249 checked, no confirmed live call site found.

notes: Whether this is actually wired into the live post-scan results UI was not conclusively confirmed in this pass — flagged as an open question for the next hands-on pass on this specific capability rather than asserted either way.

name: Scan-Finding to CVE Correlation (legacy)
description: Explicitly-commented legacy endpoint correlating scan findings to CVE threat intel, cached in D1 with expiry. A third, separate correlation engine distinct from workers/src/services/correlationEngine.js (CAP-TIH-002).

frontend.evidence: No frontend caller confirmed in this pass; workers/src/index.js's own inline comment labels the route 'legacy endpoint (scan findings -> CVE correlation)'.

test_coverage.evidence: No import-confirmed test found for workers/src/lib/threatCorrelation.js.

verification.evidence: workers/src/lib/threatCorrelation.js read directly (404 lines, 5 entry points); workers/src/index.js:3529-3537 read directly, own inline comment confirms 'legacy' status; frontend/*.html not found calling this route; workers/test/*.mjs grepped with 0 hits.

notes: Self-declared legacy by the codebase's own comment — before investing in tests or a frontend for this, confirm with the business owner whether it should be deprecated/removed in favor of workers/src/services/correlationEngine.js (CAP-TIH-002's /api/v1/correlations) rather than maintained as a third parallel implementation. Priority set to P7 (documentation: the deprecation decision itself isn't recorded anywhere) rather than a build-oriented priority, since building on top of declared-legacy code without that decision first would be wasted effort.
